top_signals.title: Gold Heads for Second Weekly Drop as War Raises Fed Hike Fears
top_signals.summary: Gold was on track for the biggest weekly loss since early June as renewed hostilities in the Middle East raised concerns that higher interest rates will be needed to contain war-driven inflation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
top_signals.summary: Oil headed for its biggest weekly advance since April as the escalating conflict between the US and Iran disrupted supply from the Middle East.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Nasdaq Futures Tumble 1.8% as Chip Rout Deepens: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Nasdaq 100 futures tumbled 1.8% as the global selloff in chipmakers gathered pace, with traders rushing to exit positions in stocks that had fueled this year’s rally but whose valuations grew increasingly rich.

top_signals.title: The Executive Ledger: Is the company car consigned to history?
top_signals.summary: The traditional executive company car used to be defined by a beautifully simple set of rules, writes motoring journalist Tim Barnes-Clay. It required a German saloon, 3.0 litres of naturally aspirated displacement, finished in metallic paint, and a designated parking space close to the boardroom doors. It was a statement of seniority, paid for by [...]
